title: Corybas merrillii
taxonomy: Corybas merrillii is classified as a species of orchid, specifically within the genus Corybas. It belongs to the family Diurideae and is native to the Philippines. The genus Corybas is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as the "hood orchids." This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other orchid species. The Diurideae family is one of the six subfamilies of the orchid family (Orchidaceae).

intro: Corybas merrillii, also known as the Philippine hood orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species native to the Philippines. Traditionally, Corybas merrillii has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
